name: FluidStack
pricing_notes: A100 80GB ~$2/hr; H100 ~$2.50/hr; on-demand + reserved; significantly cheaper than AWS/GCP

auth.notes: API key from FluidStack dashboard; Bearer token in Authorization header

performance.notes: VM provisioning 30-120s; API response <500ms; GPU instance performance depends on hardware tier

agent_readiness.notes: Low-cost GPU cloud for AI workloads. Distributed network of enterprise GPU providers. A100/H100/RTX GPUs. REST API for instance management (create, start, stop, delete). Significantly cheaper than hyperscalers for AI training/inference. Less reliability guarantees than AWS/GCP but good for batch training jobs.

gotchas: Less reliable than hyperscalers — GPU availability can vary
Limited API features vs AWS EC2 (no VPC, load balancers, etc.)
Support response time slower than enterprise GPU clouds
Documentation less complete than major providers
best_when: Cost-sensitive LLM training where 50-70% savings vs AWS/GCP matter
Batch training jobs tolerant of occasional instance unavailability
ML startups or researchers with limited GPU budget
Fine-tuning runs that don't require 99.9% availability
avoid_when: Production inference serving requiring high reliability SLA
Complex networking requirements (VPCs, load balancers, private networking)
summary: FluidStack provides low-cost GPU compute (A100/H100/RTX) for AI training and inference, typically 50-70% cheaper than AWS/GCP. Distributed GPU provider network. REST API for instance management. Best for cost-sensitive ML training workloads that can tolerate occasional availability issues. Not suitable for production inference requiring high SLA. Good alternative to RunPod and Lambda Labs.
